title: ceanothus papillosus subsp. roweanus
intro: Ceanothus papillosus subsp. roweanus, also known as Rowan's buckbrush or rowan's ceanothus, is a subspecies of flowering shrub native to the western coast of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ceanothus papillosus subsp. roweanus include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and support wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and infections.
